user_query:
I’m onboarding a new AI persona into our system. Please do the following using the AIEOS schema provided in input/persona_schema.json:

1) Validate the schema against the AIEOS standard (ensure required top-level sections like @type, standard.protocol=AIEOS, identity, psychology, and linguistics are present). Summarize the validation outcome.

2) Apply the schema as a dry run and write all proposed identity artifacts to the workspace under output/ (do not modify any workspace-level files outside output/):
   - Write the full, preserved persona data to output/entity.json (this should contain the entire original schema with no loss of fields).
   - Generate human-readable identity docs from the schema and write them to:
     • output/IDENTITY.md — include name, origin/residence, and compute both the “Creature” classification (based on neural_matrix thresholds) and “Vibe” list (combine high-scoring neural traits and style descriptors). Also include an Emoji that maps from favorites.color (blue → 🔵, green → 🟢; default 💖).
     • output/SOUL.md — include Moral Alignment and Core Values, a Neural Matrix section with one-decimal scores, standard Boundaries, and a Vibe paragraph reflecting style and neural traits.

3) Generate a simple “About Me” HTML bio page from the applied persona and write it to output/bio.html. It should clearly show the persona’s display name and overall vibe.

4) Export the current identity as a full AIEOS schema and write it to output/exported_schema.json. It should be consistent with output/entity.json.

5) Finally, write a short validation and changes report to output/validation_report.md explaining what you validated, any fixes you would have applied (if needed), and any assumptions.

Use only the files under input/ for reading and only the output/ directory for writing. Avoid absolute paths.
